diagrams.description: Political sociology examines the intersection of society and politics. This flowchart maps its logic: starting from the goal of understanding how social forces shape political power and outcomes, it integrates core dimensions (the state and its autonomy, power and domination, civil society and social capital, political culture and ideology, and political participation and social movements). These dimensions are analyzed through classical and contemporary theories (Marxist class analysis, Weberian authority, elite theory, pluralism, new institutionalism, post-structuralism). The synthesis explains key political phenomena such as democratic stability, revolution, policy-making, and the rise of populism.
